subject: Import upstream component version inspection tool
commit_message:
Import upstream component version inspection tool

This patch adds utility to check versions of binaries available in
Docker containers run on Kubernetes cluster. It has been contributed by:
kkkk-k <kkkk.k@samsung.com>

Several minor changes were made to comply with ONAP CI linter rules.

Issue-ID: INT-1571
Change-Id: Id0e4b557212dec1bf8d2bac580968d69e2cf5595
Signed-off-by: Pawel Wieczorek <p.wieczorek2@samsung.com>

Patch Set 3:

Please add license header to each and every file as LF periodically scans if all files have those

Patch Set 3:

I can see that you've provided tests for the python script which is very nice, but could you also provide tox.ini setup for tox to automatically run the tests? Then we could add CI job to automatically run them in CI gate which would be even better.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Thanks for tox setup but I only managed to notice now that tests require access to kubernetes cluster so I don't think we're able to run them in CI.
Change has been successfully merged by Bartek Grzybowski

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2
> 
> Thanks for tox setup but I only managed to notice now that tests require access to kubernetes cluster so I don't think we're able to run them in CI.

Correct, responses from kubernetes have not been mocked yet (that is why pytest is out of default envlist)
